title: DECISION: sign-with-TPM/verify-with-RustCrypto p256 is the correct test oracle
description: key.rs::sign_and_verify_with_independent_oracle signs with the TPM and verifies with the p256 RustCrypto crate as the independent oracle. This is the right design per CLAUDE.md test constraints. A contributor might suggest 'also verify with the TPM's own verify to save the p256 dependency' — the comment says 'Also confirm the TPM's own verify agrees (sanity check)' which is correct, but the PRIMARY oracle is p256, not the TPM. Do not swap primary/secondary oracles.
